data.body:
EARLY-LAND DECISION (skill exception) + regression-safety check.
Completion verifier FAILED 8d36 (and would fail 9ad9/bad4) on the AC criterion 'Change passes CI (Verified +1) and lands via Gerrit' — the 'lands via Gerrit' half is inherently post-merge, so these tickets can only be verified AFTER their change is on main. This is the skill's sanctioned early-land exception.
REGRESSION-SAFETY CHECK (passes):
 - All 3 changes green on BOTH gates: Verified +MAX and LLM-Review +MAX (Gerrit 644/645/646).
 - Blast radius: CI-config (.github/workflows/*), test-infra (tests/conftest.py leak-guard), pyproject test deps + coverage.run, docs — ZERO src/rebar runtime logic changed. No product behavior can regress.
 - Full default suite passed on the exact merge tree (my worktree HEAD = full stack): 79.94% coverage, 5884 passed / 0 failed / 0 errors; integration tier 75 passed. lint+typecheck green.
 - The one behavioral change (leak-guard ignores coverage artifacts) is a deliberate, proven relaxation; the parallelization is proven byte-identical to serial.
FFO note: origin/main advanced (ee49b4a89 -> abdf44ef7, an autolander commit) while in review, so the stack needs a rebase-and-re-CI before submit. Handing to the auto-lander via Autosubmit (it serializes the rebase+re-CI+atomic submit; main is actively advancing). Will monitor for merged / needs_rebase / lander_down (fallback = manual FFO rebase+submit).
